title: cyanella alba subsp. minor
intro: Cyanella alba subsp. minor, also known as the white rain lily, is a bulbous perennial plant species native to Southern Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Cyanella alba subsp. minor include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal purposes, with some herbalists also using it in tincture or ointment form to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
